valid_meta.principle: Accessibility audit closure must distinguish code-owned fixes from content-owned blockers.
Derive SLOs from user-visible service outcomes and observed metrics before writing objectives, budgets, or alerts.
Completion review for prompt boosting must require workflow evidence: Joyride clarification when needed, project-context exploration, clipboard copy, revision prompt, and no source-code edits.
Verify the Expo Tailwind setup by checking the actual bundler and platform-specific CSS behavior; do not equate installed packages with working universal styling.
Correlate logs, metrics, and downstream calls from the service boundary with structured fields and bounded labels.
Review telemetry changes against evidence: confirm stack and hosting assumptions, resource creation path, connection-string placement, initialization timing, and limits of verification.
Prepare release work by classifying change type, selecting the versioning path, and defining evidence required for build, metadata, typed-package, and publishing readiness.

Semantic Kernel work starts by identifying the target language from repo evidence, consulting the matching official docs and samples, then implementing async, typed plugin/function-calling code with honest verification.
Choose startup metrics from the company stage and business model first, then calculate source-backed unit economics and diagnose them against the relevant benchmark.
Confirm FlowStudio tool schemas and drive governance writes from detail records, not assumptions or list-only fields.
Repair parser behavior from concrete frame-boundary evidence while preserving strict length and corruption checks.
Reduce Dependabot pull request noise with ordered groups while keeping security updates separate and timely.
List existing integrations before creating; choose create, reuse, or update from provider/name/credential/model/function-calling evidence; capture and verify the returned integration ID.
Connect to and inspect the active Power BI semantic model before giving modeling guidance or edits.

Focused prompt repairs should act from concrete parser failures, making the smallest contract change that addresses malformed shape, missing keys, or hallucinated values, then rerunning the failing fixtures.
When a dependency cycle crosses architecture layers, remove the outward dependency through a port and wire the concrete adapter at the edge.
Integration feedback for App Store reports should validate structure, severity, evidence, assumptions, and reviewer-friction coverage before recommending any patch work.
Detect all dependency ecosystems and directory layouts before writing Dependabot YAML, using plural directories for any globbed monorepo coverage.
During implementation, actively police the Convex component boundary: component files use their own generated imports and explicit validators while app wrappers provide auth-derived data.
Plan SAST rollout as a staged security control: map compliance and languages to tools, define baseline and gating thresholds, and require verification artifacts before claiming enforcement.
List existing integrations before creating; choose create, reuse, or update from provider/name/credential/model/function-calling evidence; capture and verify the returned integration ID.
Inspect existing Python layout and runtime entry points before choosing packaging metadata, then verify built distributions from an isolated install.
Assess relationships and schema from model metadata before changing cross-filter direction or cardinality.
When a user asks to check the napkin, do the concrete file checks first and, if the PNG is missing, give the exact recovery steps instead of silently proceeding from partial data.
When migration tests already exist, strengthen them by tying assertions to deterministic seed rows and logical outputs, not by broadening vague checks.
When tracing prerequisites already exist, keep the implementation minimal while retaining import-order, env credential, and typecheck gates.
Repair Qdrant advice by separating local-mode test convenience from server-backed benchmark and production evidence.
When a prompt file is present, perform concrete edits that improve clarity and grammar while keeping the existing role, requirements, headings, and metadata intact.
Make rollout procedures executable: each phase should pair a command with an expected signal and a stop condition.
Refactor actions should be small, local, and corrected immediately when verification disproves an assumption.
Start SQL tuning from execution-plan evidence, then change query shape and indexes only where the observed scan, join, sort, or row-estimate problem supports it.
For SQL optimization planning, classify the bottleneck by query pattern and database dialect before prescribing rewrites, indexes, batching, pagination, or monitoring.

Incremental Angular migrations should preserve live delivery by bootstrapping a hybrid app, moving one vertical slice at a time, and keeping legacy routes and services bridged until tests prove the slice works.
Derive standards from concrete file evidence, resolve configuration toggles before editing, and verify generated standards plus any inconsistency fixes against observed project style.
When async tasks share external capacity, create explicit task ownership, await every task, and bound concurrency with semaphores or queues.
Choose ArgoCD or Flux topology and sync policy from repository structure, cluster count, and release risk before writing manifests.
For dbt incremental models, verify both materialization config and compiled SQL predicates; config without an is_incremental filter can still full-scan.

Risk-first prompt safety review should inspect the prompt and tests, classify concrete privacy/bias/security/effectiveness risks, apply enforceable prompt changes, and verify only the behaviors covered by tool output.
Angular migration reviews should separate strategy fit from unsafe gaps by checking hybrid bootstrap, DI bridge correctness, route preservation, cleanup timing, and verification evidence.
Modal accessibility requires naming, focus entry, focus containment, dismissal, and focus return as a single behavior set.
Create an algorithmic philosophy before implementation, then express it through a seeded p5.js system while preserving the required viewer template boundaries.
Compliance synthesis must downgrade claims that lack runtime evidence, separate full passes from partial controls, and recommend the next concrete control for each ASI gap.
Before editing C memory-management code, build an ownership table and cleanup strategy that distinguishes transferred ownership from parser-owned temporaries.
Before changing, cancelling, or triggering a flow, classify the operation and choose the safest live or store tool based on subscription tier, response shape, and human-wait states.

After a local development-client build, integrate by installing the platform-specific artifact and starting Metro with the dev-client flag, while reporting only the connection state actually verified.
Generate or refresh the WinMD cache before platform API discovery, then choose APIs from ranked local metadata rather than memory or web snippets.
Accessibility audit closure must distinguish code-owned fixes from content-owned blockers.
Reset and persistence failures should be handled honestly while keeping the tutorial moving on the correct track.
For web games, build the runtime around an explicit input-update-render loop with delta time, then verify the loop through observable browser or test output instead of assuming animation works.

For Kubernetes secret migration, plan dependency-preserving External Secrets rollout: preserve target names, scope backend auth, stage sync before deletion, and verify metadata without printing values.
Remove parameters only when the code proves they are unused or derivable, then update every internal call site and verify compilation behavior.
Keep architecture diagrams proportional to verified boundaries and revise them when file evidence contradicts a generic template.
Airflow reviews should synthesize evidence from DAG structure, imports, task semantics, and tests, separating verified risks from assumptions before recommending changes.
Airflow reviews should synthesize evidence from DAG structure, imports, task semantics, and tests, separating verified risks from assumptions before recommending changes.
Investigate the Microsoft technology with Learn MCP or mslearn before writing, then generate a hybrid skill with only stable essentials local and dynamic doc lookups for depth.
Apply concrete Dockerfile edits that use pinned bases, cacheable dependency layers, production-only runtime contents, and non-root execution.

For 0-to-1 launches, prioritize direct first-customer learning over press awareness: name the ICP, build a target-account list, guide activation manually, and judge success by replies, trials, paid pilots, and PMF signals rather than impressions.
During image edits, treat missing keys, uv failures, and authentication errors as blockers; retry only documented transient failures.
Treat the Aspire AppHost as the source of truth: inspect the existing resource graph, wire services with resource references, and verify through Aspire runtime output.

When a launch stalls, diagnose the broken layer before prescribing a fix: distinguish positioning, experience, and alignment with funnel data and customer evidence, then design one bounded two-week experiment with explicit success and kill criteria.
Before planning implementation in a brownfield project, extract current patterns from existing code and context files, then scope the plan around documented decisions and discovered constraints.
Create issues from evidence, not from the spec alone, and keep requirement-to-issue mapping one-to-one.
Match Python style tooling to the repo's observed version and formatter contract before editing code.
On Genkit errors, read common-errors first and apply the documented v1 migration pattern instead of guessing or installing deprecated packages.
When a built Python wheel fails after installation, repair the packaging declaration that matches the installed failure before changing application code.
Create an algorithmic philosophy before implementation, then express it through a seeded p5.js system while preserving the required viewer template boundaries.
Before converting code to asyncio, classify the workload and choose async, thread offload, or synchronous code based on blocking and concurrency signals.
For Copilot per-user questions, select the user metrics script for the correct account scope and format the output as a table without exposing raw JSON noise.
Treat critical production or security fixes as hotfixes from master, while checking source branch and name collisions before checkout.
When validating HADS, inspect required structure and block semantics against the actual document before recommending edits.
During TUnit migrations, preserve behavior while replacing xUnit attributes and synchronous asserts with [Test], [Arguments], and awaited Assert.That calls.

Incremental Angular migrations should preserve live delivery by bootstrapping a hybrid app, moving one vertical slice at a time, and keeping legacy routes and services bridged until tests prove the slice works.
Scope migration test planning to database-touching artifacts in the named project, then rank Oracle-specific behavior before writing the report.
Bulk span annotation should use the Arize Python SDK with the documented dataframe columns and validation enabled, after confirming the target config exists.
In active mode, choose inline verification for moderate-risk factual claims and escalate only when severe ratings appear.
When repairing an interactive generative viewer, keep the fixed template intact and fix the seeded regeneration and control wiring with browser evidence.
Use OpenAPI validation output as a locator, but verify each repair against actual routes, schemas, and security definitions.
Plan gh automation by separating safe read operations from destructive or bulk operations, using explicit repositories, noninteractive flags, and preview queries.
During planning for Power Apps Code Apps, separate stable scaffold requirements from preview-era CLI/version details and define verification boundaries before coding.
Troubleshooting annotation-config failures should branch from the specific error and verify conflicts before reusing, deleting, or recreating schemas.
For broad website design reviews, first detect framework and styling, define page and viewport coverage, and establish evidence and edit boundaries before changing code.
When asked for an Arize deep link, use the requested resource template exactly and stop to ask for browser-derived IDs when numeric path IDs would create a broken link.
Finish by reviewing the diff against the original prompt contract, explicitly checking that metadata, markdown structure, and intent survived the polish pass.
Govern agent tool execution with fail-closed policy checks before any external action, and record the decision path for auditability.
Calendar-first daily prep: fetch complete meeting metadata, fill gaps with follow-up queries, preserve existing prep files, and ground every recommendation in WorkIQ or workspace evidence.
Verify Arize evaluator tasks from real data: resolve IDs, sample spans or runs, map every template variable, and treat run status as evidence.

Review Epic PRD handoffs against artifact evidence: reject briefs that lack required sections, measurable KPIs, personas, journeys, non-functional requirements, or scope boundaries before architecture begins.
For Copilot Studio cost questions, gather usage inputs, fetch live Microsoft billing-rate content before calculating, and convert Copilot Credits to USD at one cent per credit.
Integrate visual-system work by mapping claims to evidence: contrast, dark mode, focus and disabled states, token adoption, and responsive screenshots before approving readiness.
Gate branch completion on passing tests, then present bounded integration options and execute only the selected workflow.
Secure Firebase SQL Connect operations by pairing @auth with ownership filters, @check, @redact, and transactional permission lookups.

Feature implementation planning must save a plan-only Markdown artifact with PRD traceability, Epoch monorepo paths, architecture, schema, API, frontend, security/performance, and deployment detail before code is written.

When an implementation draft contains placeholders, open decisions, or partial snippets, repair it by returning to the plan and research artifacts, then replacing ambiguity with concrete code and observable checks.
During implementation, keep tool calls outside channels and react to observed files by narrowing guidance to exact versions and patterns actually present.
When integrating release notes, classify evidence by user-visible CLI impact, use issue language for highlights, and remove empty or noisy sections.
Generate Playwright tests only after executing the scenario step by step through browser tooling and grounding selectors in observed page state.
Design Expo release automation around explicit triggers, dependency ordering, metadata readiness, and preview lanes before adding production submit jobs.
Compose governance policies with most-restrictive-wins semantics so org-wide denies cannot be reopened by narrower team or agent policies.

Before altering a Snowflake semantic view, plan from environment, metadata, and validation constraints: confirm target context, inventory comments and star-schema paths, identify missing approved labels, and define a temporary validation and cleanup sequence.
When repairing Windows API usage, confirm exact type members and async return shapes from local WinMD metadata before editing code.
When users ask for a model comparison, stay inside the requested model set and decide from the prompt file's actual workload signals rather than generic capability rankings.
Integrate web changes from measured performance, accessibility, and browser-behavior evidence, separating supported improvements from release-blocking regressions.
Before using gh for repository work, establish authentication, hostname, and repository context explicitly, then prefer JSON fields for decisions.
When public imports fail after a Python package split, repair the package interface with evidence from import errors and keep private helpers out of __all__.
Before parallel review begins, define reviewer dimensions, finding schema, deduplication rules, and severity floors so consolidation is deterministic and risk-calibrated.
Validate customer-side ownership before kickoff to prevent Week 4 ghosting; block launch activity until specific people with authority, capacity, and motivation are named.
When a semantic component leaks palette utilities, preserve its API but move color, border, and spacing decisions into theme-aware tokens with targeted verification.
Airflow reviews should synthesize evidence from DAG structure, imports, task semantics, and tests, separating verified risks from assumptions before recommending changes.
Before appending examples, compare proposed user fields to an exported schema and strip platform-managed fields so the dataset does not drift silently.
Audit Firestore rules adversarially by comparing create and update paths, tracing authority to trusted sources, and scoring concrete exploitability.
Refactor first to clarify behavior, then add teaching comments and README sections that explain the verified code path.
Sponsor reports must be repaired by removing unverified or stale links and deduplicating by funding destination, using fresh fetch responses and source-repo evidence rather than package-name guesses.
Prepare image operations by selecting resize flags from the requested visual contract: aspect-fit, exact stretch, or crop-and-extent each imply different ImageMagick commands.
When a bypass crashes, debug the side effects of the skipped branch and repair with the smallest evidence-backed edit instead of broad NOPs.
Annotation config work should start with the concrete  operation, verify the space-specific schema, and troubleshoot only from observed errors.
Use the resource inventory as the coverage baseline, then verify relationships through read-only Azure detail queries before creating the architecture document.
Accessibility audit closure must distinguish code-owned fixes from content-owned blockers.
Upgrade major dependencies as compatibility clusters, then verify dependency tree, tests, build, and user behavior before claiming success.
Select the OpenRouter image mode from observed inputs, use supported resolution and filename flags, and report only MEDIA-backed saved paths.
Shape monorepo setup from the existing repo structure and verify the task graph rather than applying a default tool template.
When acting in an Expo UI Jetpack Compose codebase, use Compose-native components and assets: Host for the tree, LazyColumn for lists, and Android XML drawables for Icon.
Determine application stack and Azure hosting from evidence first, then choose App Insights instrumentation path that matches the hosting model and avoids unsupported configuration.
Prefer native form controls over custom ARIA when the interaction matches standard HTML semantics.

Azure cost optimization must be evidence-gated: load current best practices, prove IaC exists, discover live configuration and metrics, validate pricing, and only create GitHub issues after explicit approval.
For PDF assembly and encryption, inventory inputs, perform deterministic qpdf operations in order, and verify page counts and security flags after each output.
When the Copilot metrics API denies access, explain the likely permission and policy causes and give the user concrete remediation steps instead of treating it as missing data.
A strong Arch triage answer ties remediation to observed service and journal evidence, includes reboot boundaries, and gives cleanup or rollback commands.
For Node.js instrumentation, initialize Azure Monitor after environment loading and before application modules that create servers, clients, or loggers.
For Node.js instrumentation, initialize Azure Monitor after environment loading and before application modules that create servers, clients, or loggers.
Route Learn-hosted Microsoft questions through Microsoft Learn MCP first, then fetch full pages and code samples when excerpts are not enough to answer precisely.
Classify the dominant Qdrant scaling pressure before changing topology or tuning, because data volume, throughput, latency, tenant isolation, and query volume require different tradeoffs.
Before building Spring Boot features, inspect configuration, dependencies, package shape, and test seams so the implementation uses starters, type-safe properties, profiles, and focused verification.
For preset switches, preserve customized components unless the user explicitly chooses overwrite.
Choose webapp testing strategy by runtime surface: inspect static HTML directly, but use helper-managed Playwright scripts for dynamic apps.
Review GraphQL API designs by grounding findings in schema shape, resolver behavior, pagination contracts, mutation payloads, and N plus one evidence.
Generate OpenAPI 3.1 contracts from observed implementation details, using reusable components and validating before claiming readiness.
Require both the command name and an authoritative documentation URL before drafting, and convert only source-supported behavior into tldr examples.

Finalize Ruby MCP server generation with an evidence-bounded handoff: audit required files, run tests, verify the stdio executable, inspect documentation claims, and separate verified behavior from examples or remaining checks.
Review telemetry changes against evidence: confirm stack and hosting assumptions, resource creation path, connection-string placement, initialization timing, and limits of verification.
After confirmation, fingerprint the environment and choose tiered automation that targets the bottleneck, uses installed tools, preserves review checkpoints, and supports dry runs.
Plan Qdrant migrations by inventorying existing REST semantics, selecting the official language SDK, and defining verification that separates compile tests from live vector behavior.
For repository mapping requests, prepare from the output contract backward: exact seven docs, evidence for every non-trivial claim, and explicit [TODO]/[ASK USER] handling.
Prepare Go concurrency work by proving the workload needs parallelism, defining channel ownership and context cancellation, and planning race and leak-focused tests before coding.
Modal accessibility requires naming, focus entry, focus containment, dismissal, and focus return as a single behavior set.
Preserve Clean Architecture boundaries while implementing async .NET API paths with typed configuration, DTO contracts, CancellationToken propagation, and EF Core no-tracking projections.
Integrate Stripe subscription work only from observed billing evidence: subscription state, invoice payment events, portal access, and explicit unverified gaps.
Analyze the codebase read-only first, identify runtime scope and existing tracing, and only proceed to instrumentation when the target service is clear.
Convert a specification into one scoped feature issue only after extracting requirements and checking for duplicates.
Choose component composition from existing framework and accessibility evidence before designing the API.

A Python anti-pattern review should turn checklist smells into an evidence-backed, risk-ranked plan with explicit verification gates, while rejecting generic lint-only fixes and unnecessary architecture ceremony.
Before writing dbt transformations, derive a layer-by-layer plan from existing project conventions, source contracts, and the verification selectors that will prove each layer.
When a resource group is specified, move directly into read-only Azure discovery, collect detail records for relationship evidence, and verify every discovered resource appears in the diagram.
When a bypass crashes, debug the side effects of the skipped branch and repair with the smallest evidence-backed edit instead of broad NOPs.
Keep business rules inside framework-free domain/use-case layers and verify the boundary with in-memory tests plus import checks.
For browser-facing authentication, prefer secure httpOnly cookies, input validation, and rate limiting so password and token handling resist XSS, CSRF, and brute-force pressure.

Before adding educational comments, confirm the target and configuration, preserve encoding and line endings, plan comments against a measured line-count target, then validate syntax and formatting after editing.

Drive implementation through a visible red-green-refactor loop: write one behavior test, run it to the expected failure, implement only enough production code, then verify the targeted and broader test suite before integrating.
Before dispatching subagents, the controller should decide whether tasks are independent, choose models by complexity, and define sequential review gates for each task.
During mid-incident handoff, verify live state, close ownership and escalation gaps, and post a readback before accepting command.
Criterion-first evaluator optimizer loops must separate generation, evaluation, and optimization, use structured critique, bound iterations, detect convergence, and keep history.
Ground architecture blueprints in discovered project evidence before naming patterns or layers.
Repair WinUI 3 migration mistakes from concrete compiler/runtime feedback: map each failing UWP API to the Windows App SDK equivalent instead of patching symptoms or suppressing errors.
When a Fedora service fails, inspect systemd and journal evidence first, then map the observed failure to Fedora-specific package or initialization commands.
When provider sign-in fails, diagnose provider provisioning separately from client SDK usage and respect Firebase CLI provider limits.
When a provider-only migration breaks consumers, repair by finding every stale contextTypes reader and converting them to the already-created modern context rather than restoring legacy shims.
Block premature optimization until metric, scope, constraints, and baseline are confirmed.
For dynamic content, use polite or assertive live-region semantics according to urgency, preserve focus unless the user must act, and verify announcements separately from visual updates.
When Azure pricing queries return empty or suspicious results, broaden filters deliberately to discover canonical service names and casing before calculating.
Validate requirement sets by checking threat coverage, compliance support, testability, and evidence-bounded readiness.
Integrate Convex audit results by reporting only evidence-backed improvements and naming any production metric still unverified.

When repairing STRIDE work, compare every claimed threat and mitigation against the actual diagram or implementation; fill skipped categories from concrete interactions, downgrade unsupported claims, and keep verification limited to document/schema checks.
Accessibility audit closure must distinguish code-owned fixes from content-owned blockers.

Incremental Angular migrations should preserve live delivery by bootstrapping a hybrid app, moving one vertical slice at a time, and keeping legacy routes and services bridged until tests prove the slice works.
Before building custodial DeFi contracts, map value flows, roles, invariants, and threat classes into explicit verification gates.
Prepare SLO work by separating desired reliability outcomes from missing instrumentation, then gate alerts and budget policy on measurable SLIs.
Design Airflow DAG work around idempotent, atomic, incremental tasks with explicit dependencies, retries, timeouts, and tests before treating the pipeline as production-ready.

For multimodal Firebase AI Logic flows, preserve MIME types, stream user-visible responses when latency matters, and route files larger than the inline limit through Cloud Storage instead of forcing base64.
For static-site Markdown migrations, plan around target generator semantics before bulk conversion: front matter, shortcodes, Markdown flavor, raw HTML policy, and build verification.
Choose the multi-cloud pattern from RTO/RPO, data gravity, compliance, and operational expertise instead of defaulting to active-active.
When the user supplies recipient, purpose, key points, and deadline, move directly into tone matching, concise drafting, and dated markdown output.

Optimize Bazel monorepos by reshaping the target graph before tuning execution: use fine-grained package targets, explicit deps and visibility, conservative cache policy, and verify with query/profile output.
Replace broad Kubernetes permissions with namespace-scoped, least-privilege RBAC and verify both allowed and denied permissions through impersonated authorization checks.
Final responses for educational-comment edits should report the concrete file touched, comment strategy, validation evidence, and any limits or assumptions without claiming unsupported checks.
Use Azure DevOps defaults and CLI discovery before mutating repository policy so commands target the right organization, project, repo, branch, and pipeline.
Use the scaffold and bundled workflow for complex React artifacts instead of hand-assembling multi-component HTML.
When implementing Java MCP handlers, register list and call/read/get handlers explicitly and return reactive success or error responses rather than throwing for ordinary user input mistakes.
Plan issue creation by classifying every spec requirement against concrete code evidence and duplicate-search needs.
During consolidation, repair bad merges by comparing issue mechanism and remediation, preserving co-located but distinct findings while merging true duplicates with attribution.
When Markdown rendering behaves incorrectly, repair the specific renderer option or template boundary and verify the failing construct in generated HTML.
Use list_issues with explicit assignee and all states, then react to returned issue metadata
For FAQs, prepare by finding repeated employee confusion, mapping answers to official sources, and flagging uncertain policy questions before drafting.
Verify Microsoft SDK methods against official Learn references before coding, especially when names or versions are easy to hallucinate.
A strong Arch triage answer ties remediation to observed service and journal evidence, includes reboot boundaries, and gives cleanup or rollback commands.
Discovery before prescription: for messy business inputs, classify actors, triggers, data ownership, integrations, and unknowns before committing to an architecture.

For Supabase auth and SSR work, prepare from current documentation and local code evidence before editing, especially around getUser versus getSession, JWT freshness, service keys, and app_metadata authorization.
Determine application stack and Azure hosting from evidence first, then choose App Insights instrumentation path that matches the hosting model and avoids unsupported configuration.
Screen Oracle-to-PostgreSQL migration scope against the reference index, then tie each applicable behavioral gap to concrete code, fixes, and tests.
Before appending examples, compare proposed user fields to an exported schema and strip platform-managed fields so the dataset does not drift silently.
Repair skill metadata and structure by preserving intent while fixing folder/name, discovery description, and unnecessary optional directories.

When adding Convex to an existing frontend, inspect the framework first, install only the missing package, wire the provider at the root with the framework-specific env var, and avoid hard-coded deployment values.
Confirm recent Arch updates and environment assumptions before choosing a minimal, validated systemd or pacman remediation.
Escalate high-risk verification when exact citations or precise numbers cannot be sourced.
Repair side-bar command contributions as a coherent manifest unit: special id, title, icon, ordered menu group, when clause, and command-palette exclusion must agree.
Create Expo development clients only when native-code requirements make Expo Go insufficient, then encode that decision in an explicit EAS development profile before building.
During comment insertion, repair comments that violate the configured educational style by changing only comment text and then rechecking line count and syntax.
Finish by reconciling code, comments, README, and verification evidence into a coherent beginner learning artifact.
Prefer native form controls over custom ARIA when the interaction matches standard HTML semantics.
